<Textbox Name="Textbox37"> <CanGrow>true</CanGrow> <KeepTogether>true</KeepTogether> <Paragraphs> <Paragraph> <TextRuns> <TextRun> <Value>Received subject to the classification and tarrifs in effect on the date of issue of this original Bill of Lading, or, received, subject to the rules for carriage of Express and Non-Carload Freight Traffic and tarrifs in effect on the date of issue of this original shipping contract (Bill of Lading), goods described below, in apparent good order, except as noted (contents and condition of contents of packages unknown), marked , consigned and destined as indicated below, whick company agress to carry to its usual place of delivery at said destination, if on its road, otherwise to deliver to another carrier on the route to said destination. It is mutually agreeed, as to each carrierof all or any of said goods overall or any portion of said route to destination, as to each party at any time interest in all or any said goods, that every service to be performed here under shall be subject to all terms and conditions (which are heare by incorporated by reference and have the same force and effect as if the same were severally, fully and specifically set forth herein).</Value> <Style> <FontSize>8pt</FontSize> </Style> </TextRun> </TextRuns> <Style /> </Paragraph> <Paragraph> <TextRuns> <TextRun> <Value /> <Style> <FontSize>8pt</FontSize> </Style> </TextRun> </TextRuns> <Style /> </Paragraph> <Paragraph> <TextRuns> <TextRun> <Value>1. Approved by the Canadian transport commission General order No, T-5, date February 1,1965 set forth in the Canadian Freight Classification and also available at all Railway agency stations and freight offices upon request, When said goods are carried by a rail carrier; or</Value> <Style> <FontSize>8pt</FontSize> </Style> </TextRun> </TextRuns> <Style /> </Paragraph> <Paragraph> <TextRuns> <TextRun> <Value>2.of the bill of lading of the water carrier as provided in its tarrifs of Rules and Regulations when said goods are carried by water carrier: or</Value> <Style> <FontSize>8pt</FontSize> </Style> </TextRun> </TextRuns> <Style /> </Paragraph> <Paragraph> <TextRuns> <TextRun> <Value>3.of the bill of lading set forth in or prescribed by the relevant tarrifs, classification, statuties and regulations pertaining to motor carrier's services when said goods are carried by a motoe carrier: or</Value> <Style> <FontSize>8pt</FontSize> </Style> </TextRun> </TextRuns> <Style /> </Paragraph> <Paragraph> <TextRuns> <TextRun> <Value>4 of the bill of lading form schedule "A" amended by O.C.986-79-4 April 1979 Sect 12A as approved by the Quebec Transportation board when said goods originating in Quebec are to be carried by a motor carrier.</Value> <Style> <FontSize>8pt</FontSize> </Style> </TextRun> </TextRuns> <Style /> </Paragraph> <Paragraph> <TextRuns> <TextRun> <Value>5.or approved by the Canadian transport commission General order No, T-43, set forth in the rules for the carriage of express and non-carload freight traffic and also available at all express and railway agency stations and express and freight offices upon request , when said goods are carried by a rail carrier : and which are agreed to by the shipper and accepted for himself and his assigns.</Value> <Style> <FontSize>8pt</FontSize> </Style> </TextRun> </TextRuns> <Style /> </Paragraph> <Paragraph> <TextRuns> <TextRun> <Value xml:space="preserve"> </Value> <Style> <FontSize>8pt</FontSize> </Style> </TextRun> </TextRuns> <Style /> </Paragraph> </Paragraphs> <rd:DefaultName>Textbox34</rd:DefaultName> <Top>0.69028in</Top> <Left>0.01389in</Left> <Height>1.20139in</Height> <Width>6.49848in</Width> <Border> <Style>None</Style> </Border> <PaddingLeft>2pt</PaddingLeft> <PaddingRight>2pt</PaddingRight> <PaddingTop>2pt</PaddingTop> <PaddingBottom>2pt</PaddingBottom> </Style> </Textbox> <Textbox Name="Textbox37"> <CanGrow>true</CanGrow> <KeepTogether>true</KeepTogether> <Paragraphs> <Paragraph> <TextRuns> <TextRun> <Value>Received, subject to the classifications and tarrifs in effect on the date of issue of this Original Bill of Lading, or, received, subject to the Rules for the Carriage of Express and Non-Carload Freight Traffic and tarrifs in effect on the date of issue of this original shipping Contract (bill of Lading), goods described below, in apparent good order, except as noted (contents and conditions of contents of packages unknown), marked , consigned and destined as indicated below, which said Company agrees to carry to its usual place of delivery at said destination, if on its road, otherwise to deliver to another carrier on the route to said destination. It is mutually agreeed, as to each carrierof all or any of said goods over all or any portion of said route to destination, and as to each party at any time interested in all or any said goods, that every service to be performed hereunder shall be subject to all terms and conditions (which are heareby incorporated by reference and have the same force and effect as if the same were severally, fully and specifically set forth herein),</Value> <Style> <FontFamily>Segoe UI</FontFamily> <FontSize>8pt</FontSize> </Style> </TextRun> </TextRuns> <Style /> </Paragraph> <Paragraph> <TextRuns> <TextRun> <Value>1. approved by the Canadian Transport Commission by General Order No. T-5, dated February 1,1965 set forth in the Canadian Freight Classification and also available at all Railway agency stations and </Value> <Style> <FontFamily>Segoe UI</FontFamily> <FontSize>8pt</FontSize> </Style> </TextRun> </TextRuns> <Style /> </Paragraph> <Paragraph> <TextRuns> <TextRun> <Value> freight offices upon request, when said goods are carried by a rail carrier; or</Value> <Style> <FontFamily>Segoe UI</FontFamily> <FontSize>8pt</FontSize> </Style> </TextRun> </TextRuns> <Style /> </Paragraph> <Paragraph> <TextRuns> <TextRun> <Value>2.of the bill of lading of the water carrier as provided in its tarrifs of Rules and Regulations when said goods are carried by water carrier; or</Value> <Style> <FontFamily>Segoe UI</FontFamily> <FontSize>8pt</FontSize> </Style> </TextRun> </TextRuns> <Style /> </Paragraph> <Paragraph> <TextRuns> <TextRun> <Value>3.of the bill of lading set forth in or prescribed by the relevant tarrifs, classification, statutes and regulations pertaining to motor carrier's services when said goods are carried by a motoe carrier: or</Value> <Style> <FontFamily>Segoe UI</FontFamily> <FontSize>8pt</FontSize> </Style> </TextRun> </TextRuns> <Style /> </Paragraph> <Paragraph> <TextRuns> <TextRun> <Value>4 of the bill of lading form schedule "A" amended by O.C. 986-79-4 April 1979 Sect. 12A-as approved by the Quebec Transportation Board when said goods originating in Quebec are to be carried by a </Value> <Style> <FontFamily>Segoe UI</FontFamily> <FontSize>8pt</FontSize> </Style> </TextRun> </TextRuns> <Style /> </Paragraph> <Paragraph> <TextRuns> <TextRun> <Value> motor carrier;</Value> <Style> <FontFamily>Segoe UI</FontFamily> <FontSize>8pt</FontSize> </Style> </TextRun> </TextRuns> <Style /> </Paragraph> <Paragraph> <TextRuns> <TextRun> <Value>5.or approved by the Canadian Transport Commission by General order No. T-43, set forth in the Rules for the Carriage of Express and Non-Carload Freight Traffic and also available at all express and </Value> <Style> <FontFamily>Segoe UI</FontFamily> <FontSize>8pt</FontSize> </Style> </TextRun> </TextRuns> <Style /> </Paragraph> <Paragraph> <TextRuns> <TextRun> <Value> railway agency stations and express and freight offices upon request , when said goods are carried by a rail carrier ; and which are agreed to by the shipper and accepted for himself and his assigns.</Value> <Style> <FontFamily>Segoe UI</FontFamily> <FontSize>8pt</FontSize> </Style> </TextRun> </TextRuns> <Style /> </Paragraph> <Paragraph> <TextRuns> <TextRun> <Value xml:space="preserve"> </Value> <Style> <FontFamily>Segoe UI</FontFamily> <FontSize>8pt</FontSize> </Style> </TextRun> </TextRuns> <Style /> </Paragraph> </Paragraphs> <rd:DefaultName>Textbox34</rd:DefaultName> <Top>0.68056in</Top> <Height>1.20139in</Height> <Width>9.98415in</Width>
